item.title: Come work with us! We offer another round of attractive #postdocposition|s and #PhDposition|s in #qu...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
Come work with us! We offer another round of attractive #postdocposition|s and #PhDposition|s in #quantuminformationtheory, on topics ranging from abstract #mathematical themes to pragmatically motivated questions, in an open and stimulating discussion climate. Topics include -

Jens Eisert

item.title: Today, we are holding the second project meeting of #PraktiQOM, a project funded by the @bmftr_bund...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
Today, we are holding the second project meeting of #PraktiQOM, a project funded by the @bmftr_bund (@QuantenTech) and dedicated to solving practical #optimization problems using cryptographic techniques on quantum computers. The project focuses on developing new methods and

Jens Eisert

item.title: #QEC2026 is underway. The programme looks exciting. Too bad I have to miss it (ironically, because o...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
#QEC2026 is underway. The programme looks exciting. Too bad I have to miss it (ironically, because of visa issues). We are presenting our work on “QGPU: Parallel Logic in Quantum LDPC Codes,” which demonstrates an attractive approach to implementing quantum logic with qLDPC

Jens Eisert

item.title: CC @project_q_2024.
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
CC @project_q_2024.

Jens Eisert

item.title: QGPU: Parallel logic in quantum LDPC codes We introduce #clusteredcycliccodes and show how to pursue...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
QGPU: Parallel logic in quantum LDPC codes We introduce #clusteredcycliccodes and show how to pursue highly parallelized surface-code style quantum logic with quantum low density parity check codes featuring simple logicals. https://lnkd.in/dgnkNTXm In detail,

Jens Eisert

item.title: CC @project_q_2024.
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
CC @project_q_2024.

Jens Eisert

item.title: Optimal stellar rank approximation of squeezed cat states with photon catalysis We present and caref...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
Optimal stellar rank approximation of squeezed cat states with photon catalysis We present and carefully analyze a scheme for preparing photonic cat states, motivated by efforts of preparing states for photonic quantum error correction. https://scirate.com/arxiv/2607.02427… Non-#Gaussian

Jens Eisert

item.title: Congratulations, dear Ryotaro Suzuki, for your wonderful viva on “Unitary Designs, Complexity, and I...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
Congratulations, dear Ryotaro Suzuki, for your wonderful viva on “Unitary Designs, Complexity, and Information Dynamics in Random and Noisy Quantum Systems” at the @FU_Berlin.

Jens Eisert

item.title: How hard is it to verify a classical shadow?
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
How hard is it to verify a classical shadow?

Jens Eisert

item.title: How hard is it to verify a classical shadow? We link notions of learning theory with those of comput...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
How hard is it to verify a classical shadow? We link notions of learning theory with those of computational complexity, bringing together notions of physics and theoretical computer science. I am happy to see this paper out in the volume of the 53rd International Colloquium on

Jens Eisert

item.title: Rigorous no-go theorems for heralded linear-optical state generation tasks: Algebraic geometry meets...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
Rigorous no-go theorems for heralded linear-optical state generation tasks: Algebraic geometry meets design choices in linear optical state preparation for photonic quantum computing. Concretely, we apply the Nullstellensatz Linear Algebra algorithm from algebraic geometry to

Jens Eisert

item.title: Today, Jan Naumann impressed everyone with his talk and the discussions during his viva on numerical...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
Today, Jan Naumann impressed everyone with his talk and the discussions during his viva on numerical approaches to #tensornetworks, including novel variational principles for iPEPS, automatic differentiation techniques, and exciting ideas for improving existing methods.

Jens Eisert

item.title: Witness expansion: A unified framework for analytical and measurable mixed-state resource detection:...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
Witness expansion: A unified framework for analytical and measurable mixed-state resource detection: This work presents a path towards making witnesses for various quantum resources better. https://scirate.com/arxiv/2606.27105… Quantum information science aims to harness different kinds of

Jens Eisert

item.title: 柏林自由大学2026年校园跑活动，与团队一起。
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
柏林自由大学2026年校园跑活动，与团队一起。

Jens Eisert

item.title: "Magic, muggles, measurement: A meditation on quantum resources in three readings", a talk to be giv...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
"Magic, muggles, measurement: A meditation on quantum resources in three readings", a talk to be given tomorrow at the wonderful 7th Seefeld Quantum Information Workshop. https://ritschgroup.uibk.ac.at/seefeld2026/index.htm… We have been doing a great deal of work on practical topics, including quantum

Jens Eisert

item.title: [Jens Eisert 已转帖] Nearest-neighbour gates are all you need: High-rate quantum low-density parity-check codes on a plan...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
Nearest-neighbour gates are all you need: High-rate quantum low-density parity-check codes on a planar grid This is a piece of work I am particularly happy with. It shows that many of the advantages of quantum low-density parity-check (#qLDPC) codes can be realized on planar

Jens Eisert

item.title: Experimental measurement and a physical interpretation of quantum shadow enumerators We show that Ra...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
Experimental measurement and a physical interpretation of quantum shadow enumerators We show that Rains' quantum shadow enumerators - a powerful mathematical tool in the theory of #quantumerrorcorrection - admit a direct physical interpretation in terms of outcome statistics of

Jens Eisert

item.title: Warm thanks to the dream team of Boren Gu, Tamás Noszkó, Vincent Steffan, Jens Niklas Eberhardt, Jos...
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
Warm thanks to the dream team of Boren Gu, Tamás Noszkó, Vincent Steffan, Jens Niklas Eberhardt, Joschka Roffe, and Stergios Koutsioumpas for the wonderful collaboration.

Jens Eisert

item.title: [Jens Eisert 已转帖] Ruth Lawrence (born 1971); one of the highest-IQ mathematical prodigies on record. Pictured here as...
item.author: Mathematica
item.content:encoded:
Ruth Lawrence (born 1971); one of the highest-IQ mathematical prodigies on record. Pictured here as a young girl. At age 9 she earned a Grade A in A-level Pure Mathematics. At age 10 she passed the Oxford University mathematics entrance exam, ranking first among 530 candidates

Mathematica

item.title: When you live in the forest. (But, @preskill, no bears).
item.author: Jens Eisert
item.content:encoded:
When you live in the forest. (But, @preskill, no bears).

Jens Eisert
